name: China
headline: China CONDUCT CONTINUATION CONFIRMATION at 19.5 / Critical — day-1-after-band-crossing. Xi-Putin summit underway in Beijing (May 19-20); Xi reaffirming Russia ties as 'stabilising force'; joint statement pending. NATO confirmation that China supplies 90% of Russia's dual-use technology for the Ukraine war remains the operative BND/INT context. The summit itself is dual-signal: dual-use-supply continuation reinforces the state-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply methodology category (yesterday's downgrade rationale); Xi's reported mild criticism of Putin to Trump is weak counter-evidence but does not alter material supply chain. Pre-joint-statement carry-forward; full re-assessment after May 20 statement text is available.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Uyghur forced-labor structural conditions unchanged.

BND.rationale: Sustained at post-May-18 downgrade level. State-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply methodology category active. May 19 summit reinforces but does not extend the established dimensional weight.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. No self-accountability mechanism activated regarding EU 20th sanctions package designations (April 23) or dual-use supply chain.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. Xi's reported mild criticism of Putin to Trump (Kyiv Post May 19) is weak counter-evidence but is offset by Putin-Xi public 'stabilising force' framing and continued dual-use supply. I1 consistency-under-pressure: China holds its Russia-alignment posture under direct external pressure (EU sanctions, NATO public attribution) — strong negative I1 signal sustained.

evidence.finding: Putin framing China-Russia ties as 'stabilising force in world' ahead of Xi talks May 19-20. Joint statement expected covering Ukraine, Iran, Taiwan.
Putin's Beijing visit days after Trump-Xi summit; energy and Ukraine-war discussions central; China managing balancing act between Washington and Moscow.
Xi reportedly told Trump that Putin may 'regret' the Ukraine invasion — described as the farthest Xi has gone in publicly distancing from Putin. ICC discussion reported.
'China holds the cards' — Beijing's leverage over Russia framed by analysts; balancing-act framing rather than full strategic-alliance framing.
NATO public attribution: China supplies 90% of Russia's dual-use technology for the Ukraine war. Operative material context for BND downgrade.

keyEvidence: Xi-Putin summit underway May 19-20 in Beijing.
Putin: China-Russia ties as 'stabilising force' (pre-summit framing).
Joint statement expected covering Ukraine, Iran, Taiwan.
Xi's reported mild criticism of Putin to Trump (weak counter-evidence).
NATO confirmation: China supplies 90% of Russia's dual-use technology — operative material context.
Conduct continuation post-band-crossing (yesterday's Developing → Critical apply).
rationale: Conduct continuation confirmation at 19.5. The May 18 band crossing to Critical was applied (BND 2.0 → 1.75 + dual-use-supply methodology category). Today's evidence (Xi-Putin summit framing, joint-statement pending) is consistent with that downgrade rationale but does not justify further dimensional movement before the joint statement text is available. The summit's content (May 20) will determine whether additional BND/INT downgrade is warranted (if joint statement explicitly endorses Russia's war effort) or whether the current floor-adjacent position is sustained. Math: (2+2+2.5+1.5+1.75+1+2.5+1)/8 = 14.25/8 = 1.78125 → ((1.78125-1)/4)*100 = 19.53 → 19.5.
whyHeadline: Xi-Putin summit reinforces dual-use-supply-to-war methodology category; joint statement pending May 20.

dominantDimension.note: INT floor sustained. Xi public 'stabilising force' framing offsets reported mild Trump-channel criticism; net no movement.

nextForwardSignal.label: Xi-Putin joint statement text — Ukraine language and dual-use supply commitments

watches: Any explicit dual-use supply chain commitments (further negative)
Trump-Xi follow-up signaling on China-Russia relationship
carryForward: EU 20th sanctions package (April 23) — 16 Chinese entities designated
boundaryWatchResolution: Floor-adjacent. 19.5 sits 0.5pt above Critical/Developing boundary from the wrong side (in Critical band). Further negative evidence (joint statement endorsing Russia's war) would deepen the Critical position rather than crossing a band.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
